hero.headline: NIU Online MBA in Production & Operations Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Noida International University's UGC-DEB approved online MBA with a Production and Operations Management specialisation. NAAC A+, WES recognised. Total all-in fee Rs 97,600 after 25% Early Bird discount. Supply Chain and Logistics, Project Management, TQM, Lean Manufacturing, Six Sigma, and Operations Strategy in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: NIU Online MBA Production & Operations Management: NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved, WES recognised, Rs 97,600 all-in after 25% Early Bird. Operations depth with Supply Chain and Logistics, Project Management, TQM, Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma, Operations Strategy, and Supply Chain Analytics. For manufacturing, logistics, and supply chain professionals on a budget.

about.body:
The NIU Online MBA in Production and Operations Management is offered by Noida International University, Greater Noida. NIU holds NAAC A+ accreditation and UGC-DEB approval. The degree is WES recognised.

The specialisation covers 10 dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects: Supply Chain and Logistics Management, Project Management, Production Planning and Control, Total Quality Management, Technology Innovation and New Product, Service Operations Management, Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma, Operations Strategy, Supply Chain Analytics, and Quantitative Analysis of Risk. This curriculum targets professionals in manufacturing, logistics, warehousing, and supply chain operations.

NIU is not NIRF ranked, and its operations management alumni network is limited. The programme's strength is the Rs 97,600 all-in fee with strong operations and supply chain subject coverage. For working professionals in Greater Noida's manufacturing corridor and Delhi-NCR's logistics hubs, NIU offers a cost-effective operations management credential.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply strategic frameworks, operations strategy alignment, and competitive positioning for manufacturing and service businesses.

sem3Subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
sem3Subjects.description: Study business law, contract law, factory regulations, and compliance frameworks relevant to manufacturing and operations.

sem3Subjects.name: Supply Chain and Logistics Management
sem3Subjects.description: Design end-to-end supply chains, logistics network planning, inventory management, and distribution strategy.

sem3Subjects.name: Project Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply project planning tools (PERT, CPM, Gantt), resource allocation, risk management, and project delivery frameworks.

sem3Subjects.name: Production Planning and Control
sem3Subjects.description: Design production schedules, capacity planning, material requirements planning (MRP), and shop floor control systems.

sem3Subjects.name: Total Quality Management
sem3Subjects.description: Implement TQM frameworks, quality circles, Deming principles, Kaizen, and continuous improvement methodologies.

sem3Subjects.name: Technology Innovation and New Product
sem3Subjects.description: Manage technology adoption cycles, new product development processes, and innovation management in manufacturing.

sem4Subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
sem4Subjects.description: Study manufacturing venture planning, production startup models, and operations-led business development.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Marketing and E-Commerce
sem4Subjects.description: Apply e-commerce fulfilment strategy, D2C operations, and digital supply chain management.

sem4Subjects.name: Service Operations Management
sem4Subjects.description: Apply operations management principles to service industries including banking, healthcare, hospitality, and IT services.

sem4Subjects.name: Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma
sem4Subjects.description: Implement lean principles, waste elimination, DMAIC methodology, and Six Sigma quality control for manufacturing and services.

sem4Subjects.name: Operations Strategy
sem4Subjects.description: Align operations capabilities with business strategy, make-or-buy decisions, and competitive operations positioning.

sem4Subjects.name: Supply Chain Analytics
sem4Subjects.description: Use data analytics for demand forecasting, inventory optimisation, supplier performance analysis, and logistics cost modelling.

sem4Subjects.name: Quantitative Analysis of Risk
sem4Subjects.description: Apply statistical methods, simulation models, and quantitative risk assessment to operations and supply chain decision-making.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all NIU Online MBA specialisations. Strategic Management and Legal Aspects of Business are common Sem 3 subjects. Entrepreneurship Development and Digital Marketing and E-Commerce are common Sem 4 subjects.

whoHires.body:
Production and Operations Management MBA graduates target these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Manufacturing, Automotive, FMCG (production), Logistics and warehousing, E-commerce fulfilment, Pharma manufacturing, Construction

**Entry-level roles:** Production Executive, Supply Chain Coordinator, Quality Analyst, Logistics Executive, Operations Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** Operations Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Production Manager, Quality Manager, Logistics Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Operations, Head of Supply Chain, Plant Head, Director of Manufacturing, Chief Operating Officer

**Typical hiring organisations in Delhi-NCR:** Maruti Suzuki, Honda India, Hero MotoCorp, Dabur, Mother Dairy, Delhivery, Ecom Express, Amazon India (Fulfilment), Flipkart (Supply Chain), Blue Dart, Mahindra Logistics

skills.body:
The NIU Production and Operations Management specialisation builds skills tested by manufacturing and logistics employers:

**Production and manufacturing skills:**
- Production Planning and Control: capacity planning, MRP, scheduling, and shop floor management for production manager and plant manager roles
- Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma: waste elimination, DMAIC, lean principles, and process improvement for quality and continuous improvement roles
- Total Quality Management: TQM frameworks, quality circles, Kaizen, and ISO quality systems for quality manager and quality assurance roles

**Supply chain and logistics skills:**
- Supply Chain and Logistics Management: supply chain design, logistics planning, inventory management, and distribution for SCM and logistics roles
- Supply Chain Analytics: demand forecasting, inventory optimisation, supplier analysis, and logistics cost modelling for analytics-driven operations roles

**Strategy and risk skills:**
- Operations Strategy: operations-business alignment, make-or-buy analysis, and competitive operations for senior operations leadership roles
- Quantitative Analysis of Risk: statistical risk modelling, simulation, and decision analysis for operations risk and planning roles
- Project Management: PERT, CPM, Gantt charts, resource allocation, and project delivery for project management roles

**Soft skills:** Cross-functional coordination, vendor negotiation, production team leadership, and operations performance reporting.

comparisons.body:
Comparing NIU Online MBA Production and Operations Management against peers:

**vs Galgotias Online MBA (Rs 86,400):** Galgotias is slightly cheaper and NCR-based. NIU offers 10 dedicated operations subjects including Lean Six Sigma and Supply Chain Analytics. Both lack NIRF ranking. For operations-specific careers, NIU has stronger subject depth.

**vs Uttaranchal Online MBA (Rs 94,000-98,000):** Near-identical fee bracket. Both lack NIRF ranking. NIU's Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma and Supply Chain Analytics subjects are differentiators for manufacturing careers.

**vs IGNOU MBA (Rs 66,000):** IGNOU is cheaper with government brand but has limited operations specialisation depth. NIU offers 10 dedicated operations subjects including modern analytics and lean methodologies.

**vs Amity Online MBA Operations (Rs 2,25,000):** Amity is NIRF #22 at 2.3x the cost. For operations roles at large manufacturing companies where brand matters, Amity justifies the premium. NIU wins on affordability.

**vs CU Online MBA Operations (Rs 1,65,000):** CU is NIRF #19 with PwC partnerships. CU costs Rs 67,400 more. For operations management at large corporates, CU's brand and industry links add value.

**vs LPU Online MBA Operations (Rs 1,61,000):** LPU is NIRF #31 with zero-cost EMI. NIU is Rs 63,400 cheaper. For Greater Noida manufacturing corridor professionals, NIU's location and fee advantage are practical benefits.

fees.body:
The NIU Online MBA in Production and Operations Management fee structure:

**After 25% Early Bird Discount (all-in):**
- Tuition: Rs 88,500
- Examination fee: Rs 8,000 (Rs 2,000 per semester x 4)
- Registration fee: Rs 1,100 (one-time, serves as application fee)
- Total all-in cost: Rs 97,600

**Standard fee (before scholarship):**
- Semester fee: Rs 29,500 per semester
- Examination fee: Rs 2,000 per semester
- Registration fee: Rs 1,100 (one-time)

**EMI option:**
EMI options are available through financing partners. Zero-cost EMI may not be guaranteed. Confirm the interest rate and EMI terms with the admissions team before committing.

**Scholarships:** No scholarships beyond the 25% Early Bird discount. No bundled perks or certification add-ons.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with NIU each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Noida International University (NIU) and carries the MBA designation. The Production and Operations Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is WES recognised for international credential evaluation. It is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from NIU Online MBA Production and Operations Management students. Ratings from post-completion feedback.

items.name: Rajiv
items.liked: Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma and Supply Chain Analytics were directly applicable to my auto parts manufacturing role. The Rs 97,600 fee was the cheapest NAAC A+ operations MBA.
items.disliked: NIU brand is not recognised at large automotive OEMs like Maruti or Honda for management-track hiring.
items.body: For manufacturing professionals in Noida's industrial corridor, NIU's operations MBA is affordable and relevant. The 10 operations subjects cover both production and supply chain domains. Brand recognition is limited for large company management tracks, but the degree is valid for promotions and mid-level operations roles.

items.name: Ananya
items.liked: Project Management and TQM subjects were well-structured. Fee advantage over LPU (Rs 1.61L) and Amity (Rs 2.25L) was significant.
items.disliked: EMI terms were unclear. No factory visits or simulation exercises. Career support for operations roles was basic.
items.body: Solid operations curriculum at the lowest price I found. The Quantitative Analysis of Risk subject was unique and useful. Confirm EMI interest rates before enrolling. Self-sourced job search is necessary for operations management positions.

items.name: Pankaj
items.liked: Production Planning and Control and Operations Strategy were practical. Supply Chain and Logistics Management covered end-to-end supply chain design.
items.disliked: No Six Sigma certification bundled with the programme. No industry tie-ups with manufacturing companies.
items.body: At Rs 97,600, the operations subject depth is hard to beat. The 10 subjects cover production, quality, supply chain, and operations strategy. No bundled certifications, so consider adding a separate Six Sigma certification for manufacturing career progression.

faqs.question: Does NIU Online MBA Operations cover Lean Six Sigma?
faqs.answer: Yes. Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma is a dedicated Sem 4 subject covering lean principles, waste elimination, DMAIC methodology, and Six Sigma quality control. However, the programme does not include a Six Sigma Belt certification.

faqs.question: Is NIU Online MBA Operations suitable for manufacturing professionals?
faqs.answer: Yes. The programme covers Production Planning and Control, TQM, Lean Manufacturing, and Technology Innovation, all directly applicable to manufacturing production and quality roles. Working professionals in Greater Noida's manufacturing corridor can apply subjects immediately.

faqs.question: What is the total fee for NIU Online MBA Operations?
faqs.answer: Rs 97,600 all-in after the 25% Early Bird discount. This includes Rs 88,500 tuition, Rs 8,000 exam fees, and Rs 1,100 registration fee. No additional charges.

faqs.question: Does NIU Online MBA Operations include Supply Chain Analytics?
faqs.answer: Yes. Supply Chain Analytics is a dedicated Sem 4 subject covering demand forecasting, inventory optimisation, supplier performance analysis, and logistics cost modelling using data analytics methods.

faqs.question: Is NIU Online MBA Operations NIRF ranked?
faqs.answer: No. NIU does not have a NIRF ranking. The university holds NAAC A+ accreditation and UGC-DEB approval. If NIRF ranking is important for your career goals, consider LPU (NIRF #31), CU (NIRF #19), or Amity (NIRF #22) at higher fee points.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A+ operations MBA with 10 dedicated subjects covering production, supply chain, and quality at Rs 97,600 all-in.
You are a Delhi-NCR manufacturing or logistics professional who prioritises affordability over brand premium.
You want Lean Six Sigma, Supply Chain Analytics, and Project Management subjects at a sub-1-lakh price.
consider: If large manufacturer brand recognition matters, Amity (Rs 2.25L, NIRF #22) or CU (Rs 1.65L, NIRF #19) are stronger choices for management-track hiring.
If you want a bundled Six Sigma certification, consider programmes that include certification. NIU covers Lean Six Sigma as a subject but does not provide Belt certification.
If zero-cost EMI is essential, LPU offers guaranteed zero-cost 24-month EMI. NIU's EMI terms may carry interest.
